name: A list of operating systems and stuff
description: A list of operating systems and stuff

links.title: 9front
links.description: A fork of Plan 9, designed for distributed, networked computing.

links.title: AROS
links.description: Research Operating System is a lightweight, efficient, and flexible desktop operating system, designed to help you make the most of your computer. It's an independent, portable and free project, aiming at being compatible with AmigaOS at the API level

links.title: AlmeidaOS
links.description: x86-64 OS with its own bootloader, scheduler and limited libc all written from scratch

links.title: Animal
links.description: 32 bit multithreaded operating system (formerly Gramado)

links.title: AurixOS
links.description: A small multiplatform Operating System designed for developers and creators

links.title: AquilaOS
links.description: Operating System that is designed to be POSIX compliant and mostly ISA transparent

links.title: Basekernel
links.description: A simple OS kernel for research, teaching, and fun

links.title: BoneOS
links.description: OS for everyone built by everyone

links.title: Bottlerocket OS
links.description: Linux-based OS meant for hosting containers.

links.title: Brutal
links.description: An operating system inspired by brutalist design that combines the ideals of UNIX from the 1970s with modern technology and engineering

links.title: cavOS
links.description: Amd64 operating system in C. Trying to make a full OS, with a simple and readable codebase

links.title: Charlotte
links.description: CharlotteOS kernel in Rust

links.title: ChibiOS
links.description: a complete development environment for embedded applications including RTOS, an HAL, peripheral drivers, support files and tools

links.title: Clive
links.description: A unikernel OS inspired by Plan9 and Nix developed at *Universidad Rey Juan Carlos of Madrid*

links.title: Cyjon
links.description: pure x64 assembly language operating system (related to [Fern-Night](https://github.com/CorruptedByCPU/Fern-Night/) project).

links.title: DragonOS
links.description: An x86-64 OS build from scratch.

links.title: Dreamos64
links.description: An x86-64 hobby os built from scratch

links.title: Embox
links.description: Configurable operating system kernel designed for resource constrained and embedded systems.

links.title: Ethereal
links.description: Ethereal is an advanced modular operating system with support for networking and USB as well as a GUI-enabled userspace.

links.title: Fern-Night
links.description: C language operating system (related to [Cyjon](https://github.com/CorruptedByCPU/Cyjon/) project).

links.title: Fomos
links.description: Experimental OS, built with Rust.

links.title: Genode
links.description: A FOSS operating system framework consisting of a microkernel abstraction layer and a collection of userspace components

links.title: Gloire
links.description: An OS built with the [Ironclad](https://www.nongnu.org/ironclad/) kernel (a partially formally verified kernel with a small footprint for general-purpose and embedded OSes, written in SPARK and Ada) and GNU tools.

links.title: HalideOS
links.description: experimental operating system written entirely from scratch.

links.title: HarveyOS
links.description: A distributed operating system

links.title: HelenOS
links.description: multikernel multiserver OS

links.title: Hermit
links.description: A Rust-based, lightweight unikernel

links.title: House
links.description: Haskell User's Operating System and Environment.

links.title: Hubris
links.description: Hubris is an operating system for microcontrollers developed by Oxide Computer Company in Rust

links.title: Illumos
links.description: illumos is a Unix operating system which provides next-generation features for downstream distributions (like OmniOS, OpenIndiana, and Tribblix)

links.title: Interim
links.description: Minimalist OS with concepts from Lisp machines and Plan9

links.title: JSD-OS
links.description: A small operating system for 32 bit x86.

links.title: KiddieOS
links.description: UNIX-like 64-bit operating system written in Assembly, C++.

links.title: KnightOS
links.description: for z80 calculators

links.title: KolibriOS
links.description: MenuetOS fork

links.title: Lateralus OS
links.description: Bare-metal OS written in Lateralus with its own pipeline-native programming language, compiler, and VM

links.title: L4re
links.description: Operating system and hypervisor for security/safety-critical and virtualization-enabled applications.

links.title: Mako
links.description: Hobby OS for x86 from scratch, written in C

links.title: MaslOS
links.description: A 64 bit GUI multitasking Hobby OS written mostly from scratch in C++ and a bit of C/ASM.

links.title: MeetixOS
links.description: A hobby OS written in modern C++20 which aims to be Unix-like.

links.title: MentOS
links.description: An educational 32-bit linux-like Operating System.

links.title: MenuetOS
links.description: Hobby OS supporting 32 and 64 bit, written in Assembly language

links.title: Minoca OS
links.description: General purpose OS, written in C

links.title: MollenOS
links.description: MollenOS/Vali is a modern operating system that is built with focus on abstraction and a modular design, allowing anyone to port it to any architecture. It currently targets the x86-32 and x86-64 platform

links.title: myOS
links.description: Under development and Maintenance by a single Undergraduate who just wanted his machine to execute his own instructions.

links.title: MyXomycota
links.description: Monolithic kernel in C

links.title: NESOS
links.description: An OS for the Nintendo Entertainment System!

links.title: Nanos
links.description: Unikernel that is linux-compatible, written in C

links.title: NodeOS
links.description: OS using NodeJS and Linux

links.title: Northport
links.description: Monolithic kernel and support libraries for riscv64 and x86_64.

links.title: PathOS
links.description: Hobby OS based on [MikeOS](https://github.com/mig-hub/mikeOS), written in Assembly Language

links.title: Pidi OS
links.description: Independent and minimalistic OS

links.title: PouplyOS
links.description: Simple OS for fun and learning, named after the ESISAR mascot Poulpy

links.title: PureDarwin
links.description: PureDarwin is a community project to extend Darwin into a complete, usable operating system

links.title: Qubes OS
links.description: Security-focused OS built on the Xen hypervisor, designed to isolate different compartments based on levels of trust.

links.title: RavynOS
links.description: A BSD-based OS project that aims to provide an experience like and some compatibility with macOS (formerly known as airyxOS)

links.title: Redox
links.description: written in Rust

links.title: SayoriOS
links.description: Hobby OS for x86 computers, currently under active development, written in C

links.title: Sculpt OS
links.description: Genode based OS

links.title: Serenity
links.description: Graphical Unix-like operating system for x86 computers

links.title: SimpleOS
links.description: Simple Operating System coded in C and Assembly

links.title: Snowdrop OS
links.description: a homebrew operating system from scratch, in assembly language

links.title: Sortix
links.description: Hobby OS in C and C++ by Jonas 'Sortie' Termansen

links.title: Stanix
links.description: An open source modular hobby OS in C, with features such as dynamic linking and a page cache.

links.title: Syllable
links.description: Successor of AtheOS, Syllable is designed in the tradition of the Amiga and BeOS, but built using many parts from the GNU project and Linux.

links.title: SynestiaOS
links.description: 32/64 bit operating system written in C for arm platform

links.title: TempleOS
links.description: Religious OS :-)

links.title: Theseus
links.description: A modern experimental OS written from scratch in Rust to explore novel OS structure and state management techniques.

links.title: Thor
links.description: 64bit operating system mostly written in C++

links.title: ToaruOS
links.description: Hobby operating system from scratch

links.title: Týndur
links.description: Hobby operating system build by the Lowlevel community. Written in C and Pascal

links.title: Ultron OS
links.description: x86 Operating System written in C++, High School Project

links.title: Unikraft
links.description: A modular unikernel for specialization, high efficiency, performance, and security; Linux/POSIX-compatible; mostly in C

links.title: Vinix
links.description: Vinix is an effort to write a modern, fast, and useful operating system in the V programming language

links.title: Visopsys
links.description: Open Source Hobby OS developed since 1997

links.title: Windows 95 in Electron
links.description: Hobby Windows 95 implementation in Electron

links.title: Xv6
links.description: A teaching operating system developed in the summer of 2006 for MIT's operating systems course

links.title: ZealOS
links.description: The Zeal Operating System is a modernized, professional fork of the 64-bit Temple Operating System, TempleOS.

links.title: ackOS
links.description: A simple 64-bit operating system.

links.title: aurora_os
links.description: Written in Rust.

links.title: dahliaOS
links.description: dahliaOS is a modern, secure, lightweight and responsive operating system, combining the best of GNU/Linux and Fuchsia OS.

links.title: duckOS
links.description: A hobby UNIX-like OS with a graphical window manager for x86 computers.

links.title: eggos
links.description: A Go unikernel running on x86 bare metal

links.title: emerald
links.description: An operating system kernel written for fun in C

links.title: gopher-os
links.description: A proof of concept OS kernel written in Go

links.title: hhuOS
links.description: hhuOS is a small operating system written in C++ and Assembler for the x86-architecture. The main purpose of this project is to show how different aspects of operating systems theory can be implemented and linked together. The system is not aimed to be a full-featured operating system for daily use.

links.title: hydrogen
links.description: toy OS. 64-bit, preemptive multitasking kernel supporting EFI, PCIe, SATA, framebuffer graphics mode, HFS+.

links.title: lyre
links.description: x86 kernel and distribution powered by mlibc, GNU userland tools, and other common *nix software.

links.title: managarm
links.description: Pragmatic microkernel-based OS with fully asynchronous I/O

links.title: menix
links.description: A minimal and expandable Unix-like operating system.

links.title: moros
links.description: MOROS is a hobby operating system written in Rust for the x86 architecture.

links.title: mOS
links.description: A hobby operating system developed from scratch

links.title: nopeos
links.description: Simple OS kernel with BASIC interpreter for x86

links.title: octox
links.description: Unix-like OS in Rust inspired by xv6-riscv

links.title: oneOS
links.description: x86-32 and ARMv7 kernel with pre-emptive multi-threading, window manager and editor

links.title: opuntiaOS
links.description: an operating system targeting x86, ARMv7, Aarch64.

links.title: orange_slice
links.description: A research kernel and hypervisor attempting to get fully deterministic emulation with minimum performance cost. In Rust

links.title: Ovation
links.description: A simple, hobby, 64-bit operating system that focuses on simplicity, portability and maintainability. Written in C.

links.title: pranaOS
links.description: A unix operating system written from scratch in c / c++

links.title: r3
links.description: A hobby x86_64 Operating System kernel written in Rust -- with minimal functionalities

links.title: soso
links.description: Simple unix-like operating system written in Nasm assembly and mostly in C

links.title: tilck
links.description: A Tiny Linux-Compatible Kernel.

links.title: unox-os
links.description: Unox is an educational unix-like operating system

links.title: willOS
links.description: A minimal 64 bits kernel (Operating System that cannot "operate" a lot of things)

links.title: Darwin XNU
links.description: The XNU kernel source code for use in MacOS and iOS

links.title: FreeDOS
links.description: DOS compatible OS

links.title: Haiku
links.description: BeOS inspired OS

links.title: Linux
links.description: Linux kernel

links.title: MS-DOS
links.description: The original sources of MS-DOS 1.25 and 2.0

links.title: Plan 9 from Bell Labs
links.description: An OS from the creators of Unix, extending the Unix philosophy of "everything is a file" with a network-centric file system, namespaces and distributed computing.

links.title: SystemV
links.description: AT&T UNIX System V Source Code

links.title: How to Make a Computer Operating System
links.description: in C++

links.title: Intermezzos
links.description: A follow along book to build IntermezzosOS in Rust

links.title: Linux Kernel in a Nutshell
links.description: Covers the entire range of kernel tasks, available as chapters or one PDF

Think OS
